rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WILLIAMS VS. SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA TEH NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Re: Plaintiff To Submit To An Independent Metal/Psychiatric Examination
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA THE NONPROFIT ALLIANCE OF CONSUMER ADVOCATES, A BUSINESS ENTITY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RE: PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT TO AN INDEPENDENT METAL/PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATION is OFF CALENDAR for department 501. Discovery motions in general civil actions are heard in departments 301 (odd numbered cases) and department 302 (even numbered cases). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MAYU IZUMIDA ET AL VS. GAETANI REAL ESTATE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF MAYU IZUMIDA, JOSHUA STOLL MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff's motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1324 (a)(3) and (b)(3)-(4). The Court notes that in the Memorandum of Points and Authorities (not in the Motion as required) Plaintiff attempted to comply with CRC 3.1324(a)(3) by listing additions as "Page 19, Paragraphs 138 to 145: Page 20, Paragraphs 146 to 150;" however the First Amended Complaint attached to Popal Declaration is 15 pages and 107 paragraphs long. The Court further notes that Plaintiff's proposed amendment fails to separately identify distinct causes of action for misrepresentation and concealment. If Plaintiff files a Motion for Leave to Amend, Plaintiff shall clearly number and label each cause of action and allege facts in good faith in support of each element of these distinct claims. It also appears from the reply that Plaintiff plans on asserting a claim seeking to invalidate a previously entered into settlement agreement. This is not mentioned in the currently proposed amendment. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FIDE MELENDEZ ET AL VS. RSA PREMIUM FINANCE COMPANY, A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT LOANCUTTERS INC. DBA EVOQUE LENDING, MEHDI LUHRASSEBI, LEE KULICK, MICHAEL ARNOLD DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, advancing any theory of liability that is not barred by the statute of limitations. Based on the arguments advanced in the opposition, Plaintiff appears to rely on fiduciary relationship exception, fraud or conspiracy. None of these theories are supported by facts. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to plead facts. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN CAIN VS. MICHELLE JESTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 25, 2025 line 9. DEFENDANT MICHELLE JESTER , AN INDIVIDUAL DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to address the changes in Ex. 2 made without any explanation int eh first amended complaint after the demurrer to the complaint raised an issue with the contents of Ex. 2.
